because.text: Adjudicates the warrant-repo fixes from the Fable 5 Sigma-GLYPH v0.6.6 review (subject: the review doc). warrant-go is the third independent Book I evaluator, so its conformance is in scope. P0-a: R-S cost was narrowed to uint32 -- 1+size(z) wraps at 2^32, making an unaffordable step look affordable and splitting warrant-go from Python (bignum) and Rust (u64); Book I s3.4 mandates ATP Exhausted over 2^32-1. Fixed in uint64, narrow after the affordability check; verified no other uint32 cost/size narrowing survives (force cost is bounded <=3 by construction). P1: removed sigmaEval's size-1<=spent fence, which returned a canonical DISSONANCE(ATP) on a theorem and would have laundered the P0 wrap into a canonical outcome (Book I s3.6). P0-b: verify_sig accepted small-order/non-canonical Ed25519 pubkeys -- an all-zero key + all-zero sig verified for ~17/64 messages (Python) vs ~16/64 (Go), a consensus split and an actor-spoof without a keyring; both impls now reject the 8 torsion encodings + non-canonical y>=p (byte/integer-only, agree by construction). This also de-flaked negative.py, whose only-invalid-sig case had used an all-zero key. cmd@v1 check re-runs conformance PY+GO, selftest, the full suite, and warrant-go sigma-conformance 33/33; transcript pinned. Filed in sigma-glyph as 49cdeec6.

subject.note: Sigma-glyph review, warrant side: fixed warrant-go Book I R-S uint32 overflow (P0), removed a non-normative memory fence (P1), and rejected small-order Ed25519 keys in both impls (P0). Suite green.
